name: GaugeFlow exact synthetic rank-three tensor-control v1
purpose: Verify the tensor representation, Cartesian/irrep conversions, direct baseline, and orbit-alignment plumbing on an exactly evaluable nonzero equivariant target before drawing conclusions from a real-data tensor oracle.

teacher.definition: e_g = sum_{i->j in closest-image directed edges of g} (a_j-a_i) exp(-r_ij/r0) n_ij outer n_ij outer n_ij
teacher.species_scalar: fixed dense chemical token plus one; no target tensor is supplied as an input
teacher.why_not_naive_bond_sum: A symmetric directed weight times n outer n outer n cancels exactly between i->j and j->i. The antisymmetric weight is required for a nonzero odd-rank equivariant teacher.
teacher.not_a_physical_claim: This is not a piezoelectric constitutive model and cannot be used for material-property claims.

required_preflight: teacher nonzero on every heterogeneous selected structure
Cartesian SO(3) equivariance under a fixed rotation panel
representative rotations preserve the constructed orbit

comparison.fairness: All methods use the same repaired categorical/geometry substrate only after decoration-only qualification. The historical two-contraction direct_irrep is not a fair new baseline.

promotion_rule: A positive synthetic control validates only implementation plumbing. It does not reactivate real-data GaugeFlow until raw JARVIS provenance, v2 split activation, external-oracle qualification, and the preceding generator-substrate gates are separately satisfied.
